title: centaurea sonchifolia subsp. maritima
intro: Centaurea sonchifolia subsp. maritima, also known as coast artichoke or blue thistle, is a plant species that belongs to the sunflower family and is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds. The health benefits of Centaurea sonchifolia subsp. maritima include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, tinctures, and teas, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
